parsed_review.summary: CP00-371 (RP12.P02.M07.S03-S12, risk A, 10 units, "Test And Golden Case Set", deliverables 6 implementation / 2 security_audit / 2 ui) is a descriptor-only billing-core p02 test slice. Verified end-to-end consistency: (1) Plan binding — closeout-pack-plan.json CP00-371 entry has exactly 10 RP12 units S03-S12 with deliverable split 6/2/2 and all micro_title "Test And Golden Case Set", matching the manifest plan_binding_snapshot, the review baseline included_units, and the prompt's deliverable/micro_title distributions. (2) Implementation — registry.js pack binding/requirements/no-write attestation, service.js case-set/descriptor (1 section, 10 descriptor-only rows), and validators.js coverage/descriptor/Hermes/Claude/handoff functions are mutually consistent; all 7 required public exports exist and are re-exported via index.js. (3) Contract — billing-core-contract.json current_pack, p02_test_slice_descriptor (10 rows, each descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false), no_write_attestation, cp371_requirements, and historical_pack_bindings tail all match the generator output that validate-rp12-billing-core-contract.mjs deep-equals. (4) Tests — model.test.js carries CP00-371 plan-binding, descriptor-only, and evidence/handoff authority-boundary tests. (5) No-write/no-real-data — no writeFile/exec/spawn/fetch/network/process.env in billing src, test, or validator; all structures are Object.freeze descriptors; attestation flags (writes_product_state, dispatches_*_runtime, loads_real_fixture_data, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt, etc.) are false and command-evidence records no_real_data=true. (6) Authority boundary — claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, human_final_approval_required=true; manifest is correctly in pre-review state (status implemented_pending_review, production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, review pending). (7) Hermes boundary — H12 used only for deterministic evidence-packet/receipt shape, no_hermes_runtime_receipt=true, hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false. (8) Review hardening — request pins claude-opus-4-8/max/read_only with tools Read,Grep,Glob; readiness auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1455-byte valid-JSON stdout, no auth failure); validation-report confirms prompt excludes full diff/source, source_inspection_mode=read_tools_used, summary_only disallowed (security/audit present), no forbidden tools. No P0/P1/P2 defects. One P3 transparency note records that the recorded validation commands were corroborated statically rather than re-executed, consistent with the read-only reviewer role. Claude remains a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approver; enterprise-trust/runtime-opening authority stays with human approval and downstream adjudication/inspection.

findings.title: Local validation commands corroborated statically, not re-executed (read-only review boundary)
findings.details: command-evidence.json records 24 commands (node --check/--test of the changed billing files, rp05-rp12 core validators, closeout-pack-plan/weighted/spec/control-plane/goal/fullplan validators, npm test, npm run validate, npm run build, git diff --check) all with exit_code 0, plus the H12 Hermes gate as passed. As a read-only reviewer restricted to Read/Grep/Glob with no Bash, I did not re-execute these commands. I instead corroborated the production-readiness posture statically: the CP00-371 plan pack, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, registry/service/validators code, billing-core-contract.json sections (p02_test_slice_descriptor, current_pack, no_write_attestation, cp371_requirements, historical_pack_bindings tail), README entry, and model.test.js assertions are mutually consistent and match the generator output that validate-rp12-billing-core-contract.mjs deep-equals, and grep confirmed no write/exec/network/real-data paths in the billing package. The production_ready boolean is correctly false (descriptor layer; production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification marker, not a runtime-ready claim). This note documents the verification boundary for transparency; it is not a pack defect.
findings.recommendation: No action required. Treat command-evidence exit codes as recorded local evidence; final closeout validation re-run, adjudication, construction inspection, and human approval remain the authority for any runtime-opening or enterprise-trust claim, which this descriptor-only pack does not assert.
findings.post_review_disposition: informational; accepted; non-blocking — inherent to the defined read-only reviewer role
